title: Geist Design System
summary: Vercel's public foundation and component system providing typeface, React building blocks, and consistent web experience primitives. Useful for component anatomy, token naming, and responsive behavior evidence.
lessons: Study component anatomy and token naming conventions for monochrome-first systems.
Use Geist as evidence for responsive behavior and live example patterns alongside existing Vercel interface rules.
caveats: Geist is tightly coupled to Vercel's monochrome brand recipe; keep brand-specific choices separate from universal rules.
No reliable page-level update date is exposed on the documentation.
licenseNote: Public documentation; Geist font software is published under SIL Open Font License 1.1. Verify documentation and non-font asset terms separately.
antiImitationNote: Transfer component structure and token patterns; do not copy Vercel's monochrome brand identity or treat its open font as a universal brand choice.
